baseline_policy: Each candidate is compared against the shortest English baseline that preserves the same operational or semantic information. Bare terse English is not a fair baseline when it drops precision channels, evidence class, alternatives, or disambiguating anchors.

ideas.name: symbol-table amortization
ideas.hypothesis: Handles lose in one-shot messages but can win when long referents recur across a session.

ideas.name: whole-phrase admission
ideas.hypothesis: Admit constructs by whole-message or phrase savings, not by glyph-level cost alone.

ideas.name: domain packs
ideas.hypothesis: Small declared domain grammars beat generic terse English on repeated domain workflows.

ideas.name: dense tables
ideas.hypothesis: Repeated fact shapes compress better as rows than as sentences or bullets.

ideas.name: lossless elision defaults
ideas.hypothesis: Grammar-level defaults can omit recoverable verbs, subjects, and branches without dropping meaning.

ideas.name: tokenizer-native operators
ideas.hypothesis: Operators chosen from tokenizer-native substrings beat dotted handles and sigil clusters.

ideas.name: controlled abbreviation glossary
ideas.hypothesis: Declared abbreviations can safely beat English after enough repetitions.

ideas.name: precision channels
ideas.hypothesis: Tokenese beats equally precise English when evidence, confidence, alternatives, and repair state are mandatory.

ideas.name: distribution-first assertions
ideas.hypothesis: Ranked probabilistic alternatives are more compact and more faithful than English hedges.

ideas.name: semantic-neighborhood handoff
ideas.hypothesis: Near/far anchors and anti-senses can reconstruct an intended local concept neighborhood across model families.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: probabilistic semantic packets
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Highest strategic fit: improves accuracy over English while preserving compression against equally precise prose.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: amortized domain session
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Early token counts show strong savings when handles, a domain pack, and a glossary share setup overhead.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: distribution-first assertions
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Large measured savings against equally precise English and strong alignment with the probabilistic-language premise.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: symbol-table amortization
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Potentially strong for long repeated referents; weak for one-shot prompts.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: precision channels
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Measured win against equally precise English; directly supports the more-accurate-than-English goal.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: semantic-neighborhood handoff
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Measured token win and high strategic value; model-behavior validation is the critical unknown.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: whole-phrase admission
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Good admission discipline, but more of a governance rule than a standalone language feature.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: domain packs
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Useful when session scope is stable; weaker alone than when paired with handles and glossary.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: controlled glossary
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Likely needs repetition to beat glossary overhead; still valuable when paired with domain packs.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: lossless defaults
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Current fixture only breaks even; value depends on mechanically recoverable defaults and behavioral safety.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: tokenizer-native operators
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: Necessary substrate improvement, but unlikely to create large wins alone.

suspected_payoff_rank.label: dense repeated-shape payloads
suspected_payoff_rank.reason: The first compact table fixture lost to terse English; retest at larger row counts before treating this as a win.

synergy_groups.label: probabilistic semantic packet
synergy_groups.hypothesis: Evidence, rank distributions, and near/far sense anchors together may beat English on both accuracy and tokens.

synergy_groups.label: amortized domain session
synergy_groups.hypothesis: A domain pack plus handle table plus glossary should cross break-even faster than any single one.

synergy_groups.label: tokenizer-native grammar redesign
synergy_groups.hypothesis: Whole-phrase admission, defaults, and audited cheap operators should remove the known v0.3 syntax penalty.

synergy_groups.label: domain fact table with precision lanes
synergy_groups.hypothesis: Structured rows plus evidence/confidence lanes may beat terse bullets without dropping uncertainty.

synergy_groups.label: session state table
synergy_groups.hypothesis: Repeated subject handles, row shape, and recoverable defaults combine well for multi-step status exchange.

synergy_groups.label: precision distribution
synergy_groups.hypothesis: Evidence/confidence lanes plus ranked distributions should beat equally precise prose before adding semantic-neighborhood anchors.

synergy_groups.label: anchored precision
synergy_groups.hypothesis: Evidence/confidence lanes plus near/far anchors should disambiguate sense more compactly than prose.

synergy_groups.label: handle glossary
synergy_groups.hypothesis: A handle table and abbreviation glossary should amortize together on repeated referents.

cases.description: Repeated long referent over a short session; candidate must include handle declaration overhead.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: Check pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu Supabase edge-function deploy status. If pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu Supabase edge-function deploy failed, get pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu Supabase edge-function deploy logs first error with timestamp. Then summarize pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu Supabase edge-function deploy owner and retry state.

forms.label: candidate_tokenese
forms.text:
@a=pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu supabase edge-fn deploy
get @a status
if fail -> get @a logs first-error+ts
then get @a owner retry-state

cases.description: Measure phrase-level constructs as whole semantic units rather than isolated glyphs.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: If tests fail, stop and report the first failing test with timestamp. If tests pass, build and deploy production.

forms.label: candidate_tokenese
forms.text: tests fail -> stop report first-fail+ts; pass -> build deploy prod

cases.description: A small declared devops pack over several operations.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: For the API service in production, check CI, deploy status, and logs. If CI failed, return the first log error with timestamp. If severity is at least 2, roll back production.

forms.label: candidate_tokenese
forms.text:
^pack:devops
svc api prod
ci? deploy? logs?
ci:fail -> logs:first err+ts
sev>=2 -> rollback prod

cases.description: Repeated status facts encoded as a compact table.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: API is blocked; Sam owns logs. UI passed; Alex owns ship. Docs are risky; Lee owns review. Worker failed; Jo owns rollback.

forms.label: candidate_table
forms.text:
item,status,owner,next
api,blocked,sam,logs
ui,pass,alex,ship
docs,risk,lee,review
worker,fail,jo,rollback

cases.description: Candidate relies on declared defaults: query by default, same subject, fail branch inherits previous operation.

forms.label: explicit_terse_english
forms.text: Query Supabase edge-function deploy status. If the deploy failed, query the first log error with timestamp. If the deploy passed, report ready.

forms.label: candidate_tokenese
forms.text:
^default:? same-subject fail-branch
supabase.edgefn.deploy status
fail: logs.err[0]+ts
pass: ready

cases.description: Compare old dotted/sigil syntax against cheap ASCII operator alternatives.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: Check Supabase edge-function deploy status. If failed, return first log error with timestamp.

forms.label: old_v03_syntax
candidate_operator_syntax
forms.text:
svc=supabase edgefn deploy
svc -> status
svc ok? no: logs err[0]+ts

cases.description: Abbreviations include declaration overhead and must be recoverable from the glossary.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: Check the edge function deploy. If the edge function deploy failed, return the edge function error with timestamp. If edge function severity is at least 2, roll back the edge function deploy.

forms.label: candidate_tokenese
forms.text:
^abbr ef=edge-function, ts=timestamp, sev=severity
chk ef deploy
fail -> ef err+ts
sev>=2 -> rollback ef deploy

cases.description: English baseline preserves evidence class, confidence, alternatives, and action ranking.

forms.label: equal_precision_english
forms.text: Observed status is up with confidence 8 of 9. The inferred cause ranking is OOM first at 6, disk second at 3, and network third at 1. The guessed next action ranking is restart at 5, inspect logs at 4, and rollback at 2. If the receiver is unsure about the timestamp slot, it must ask for repair in plain English.

forms.label: candidate_tokenese
forms.text:
status:up ev:obs confidence:8/9
cause[oom:6 disk:3 net:1] ev:guess
act[restart:5 logs:4 rollback:2] ev:guess
repair:timestamp -> plain

cases.description: Distribution syntax makes ranked alternatives primary rather than prose hedges.

forms.label: equal_precision_english
forms.text: For cause, assign OOM weight 6, disk weight 3, and network weight 1 based on observation. For fix, assign restart weight 5, inspect logs weight 4, and rollback weight 2 as a guess.

forms.label: candidate_distribution
forms.text:
cause[oom:6 disk:3 net:1]@obs
fix[restart:5 logs:4 rollback:2]@guess

cases.description: Near/far anchors should reconstruct intended local concept neighborhoods without shared embeddings.

forms.label: disambiguating_english
forms.text: Use guardrail to mean a safety policy constraint, not a user-interface rail, physical railing, or mere preference. Treat it as closer to a binding policy than to optional guidance.

forms.label: candidate_neighborhood
forms.text:
guardrail ~= safety-policy !~= ui-rail !~= physical-rail !~= preference
near:binding-policy far:optional-guidance

cases.description: Combined precision, distribution, and semantic-neighborhood payload.

forms.label: equal_precision_english
forms.text: Use guardrail to mean a safety policy constraint, not a UI rail or preference. Observed evidence supports the policy sense at weight 7, the compliance-control sense at 6, the preference sense at negative 5, and the UI-rail sense at negative 8. Confidence is 8 of 9.

forms.label: candidate_packet
forms.text:
guardrail ~= safety-policy !~= ui-rail !~= preference
ev:obs ^8
sense[policy:7 compliance-control:6 preference:-5 ui-rail:-8]

cases.description: Domain pack plus handle plus abbreviation glossary over a repeated operational session.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: For pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu production edge function, check deploy status, check CI, check logs, and check severity. If pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu production edge function CI failed, return first pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu production edge function log error with timestamp. If pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu production edge function severity is at least 2, roll back pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu production edge function.

forms.label: candidate_session
forms.text:
^pack:devops
^abbr ef=edge-function, ts=timestamp, sev=severity
@p=pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu prod ef
?p deploy ci logs sev
ci:fail -> logs:first err+ts
sev>=2 -> rollback @p

cases.description: Whole-phrase admission plus recoverable defaults plus cheap operators.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: Check Supabase edge-function deploy status. If failed, return first log error with timestamp.

forms.label: old_v03_syntax
candidate_redesign
forms.text:
^d:? same fail
supabase edgefn deploy status
fail -> logs err[0]+ts

cases.description: Domain table with evidence and confidence lanes.

forms.label: equal_precision_english
forms.text: For API, observed status is blocked with confidence 8 and Sam owns logs. For UI, observed status passed with confidence 7 and Alex owns ship. For docs, guessed status is risky with confidence 5 and Lee owns review. For worker, observed status failed with confidence 8 and Jo owns rollback.

forms.label: candidate_table
forms.text:
^pack:status
item,status,ev,^,owner,next
api,blocked,obs,8,sam,logs
ui,pass,obs,7,alex,ship
docs,risk,guess,5,lee,review
worker,fail,obs,8,jo,rollback

cases.description: Repeated subject handle plus row-shaped state plus recoverable defaults.

forms.label: explicit_terse_english
forms.text: For pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu Supabase edge function deploy, query status, owner, retry state, and next action. If status is failed, query first log error and timestamp. If retry state is blocked, report owner and next action.

forms.label: candidate_state_table
forms.text:
^default:? same-subject
@a=pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu supabase edgefn deploy
field,val
status,?
owner,?
retry,?
next,?
status:fail -> logs.err[0]+ts
retry:blocked -> owner+next

cases.description: Pair evidence/confidence channels with ranked distributions without semantic-neighborhood anchors.

forms.label: equal_precision_english
forms.text: Observed evidence with confidence 8 of 9 supports OOM at weight 6, disk at weight 3, and network at weight 1. The guessed fix ranking is restart at 5, inspect logs at 4, and rollback at 2.

forms.label: candidate_pair
forms.text:
ev:obs ^8 cause[oom:6 disk:3 net:1]
ev:guess fix[restart:5 logs:4 rollback:2]

cases.description: Pair evidence/confidence channels with semantic-neighborhood anchors.

forms.label: equal_precision_english
forms.text: Use guardrail to mean a safety policy constraint, not a UI rail or preference. This sense is observed in the current policy text with confidence 8 of 9.

forms.label: candidate_pair
forms.text:
guardrail ~= safety-policy !~= ui-rail !~= preference
ev:obs ^8 src:policy-text

cases.description: Pair handles with declared abbreviations over repeated operational references.

forms.label: terse_english
forms.text: Check pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu production edge function deploy status. If pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu production edge function failed, return pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu production edge function error with timestamp.

forms.label: candidate_pair
forms.text:
^abbr ef=edge-function, ts=timestamp
@p=pcegwmrsxzzznowwnksu prod ef deploy
check @p status
fail -> @p err+ts
